_README: AUTHORED columns for ENGINE_MAP.md. Never generated, never overwritten by scripts/engine_map.mjs.
purpose — one sentence. If a module needs two, it is probably two modules.
playerVisibleSurface — the control, receipt, panel or prose the PLAYER actually meets, or NONE.
NONE is the L1 flag: a module with NONE, no content dependency and no GM verb is the exact shape
that produced the eight built-and-unreached capabilities. A different question from reachability.
whatMakesItFire — SNG-183 L2, 'permission isn't initiative'. What actually TRIGGERS this module's
capability in play: an always-on context block, a player control, a GM op, a threshold crossing.
Write NONE only for pure libraries nothing 'fires'. NONE on a module that HAS a player surface is
the flag that matters — the teacher gate that decided what you could learn and never made a
teacher act. It is the judgement no other gate catches and the one this batch most needed.
All THREE must be present or all absent. Backfill is incremental (ROUND 2 §5): a module earns its
three lines when a spec touches it. The gate WARNS on an undescribed module and FAILS on a partly
described one, because a partial description reads as complete.

namematch.js.purpose: The one name-resolution primitive — slugify and fuzzy-match an entity the same way everywhere, so codex, quests and inventory can never disagree about what the GM just named.
namematch.js.playerVisibleSurface: Indirect but constant: every id the player sees, and the quest panel's "couldn't match" note when the GM names something that does not resolve.
namematch.js.whatMakesItFire: Always — every entity the GM names is resolved through it on the turn it is mentioned.

quests.js.purpose: Quests as typed state the GM drives through clamped ops — the model proposes, the engine adjudicates and refuses in the open.
quests.js.playerVisibleSurface: The quest panel: active quests, stage progress lines (✦ …), the resolution choice when every stage is done, and refusal notes when the GM names the wrong stage.
quests.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM stageOps/questUpdates op, or the quest-log 'Take it on' button. SNG-162 gave it a play-time trigger; before that a structured quest could only be closed from the fix button.

playerprofile.js.purpose: Player identity across characters, and the earned play-style (tendencies, aptitudes) that accrues on each character from how it is actually played.
playerprofile.js.playerVisibleSurface: The profile screen's play-style readout, and the character-select roster.
playerprofile.js.whatMakesItFire: Every resolved action accrues play-style; the profile screen and character-select read it.

art.js.purpose: Images for characters, locations, items, NPCs and big moments, from either a static file or a generated prompt, behind one seam.
art.js.playerVisibleSurface: Every image in the app: location art, portraits, item art, the moment gallery.
art.js.whatMakesItFire: Any record without an image, at first render or on a bond crossing (ensureBondPortraits) — never eagerly, so a passing face costs nothing.

npcs.js.purpose: NPC permanence — everyone met, authored or invented mid-scene, gets a durable registry entry the GM writes to through clamped npcUpdates.
npcs.js.playerVisibleSurface: The people list and each NPC's detail card: who they are, where you met, what has passed between you.
npcs.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM npcUpdates op writes the registry; the people list and detail card read it. NB (SNG-179): the mark-a-relationship ops fire far less than the list-shaped ones.

traditions.js.purpose: The Great Circle geometry — the 24-station ring, neighbours, antipodes and distances — read from content, never hardcoded.
traditions.js.playerVisibleSurface: The skill wheel's ring layout and the tradition labels shown on abilities and standing.
traditions.js.whatMakesItFire: Always — the ring geometry is read whenever an ability, domain or standing is shown.

skilltree.js.purpose: Skill legibility and gating: tiers, attribute gates, and what is learnable now versus later.
skilltree.js.playerVisibleSurface: The skill wheel and skill graph — tier rings, locked/unlocked state, and the reason a locked ability is locked.
skilltree.js.whatMakesItFire: Opening the skill wheel or graph; a learn/deepen action re-reads it in place.

company.js.purpose: The unified company: companion, trainer, liaison, partner and ally are stacking ROLES a person in your party holds, each wired to the system that already implements it.
company.js.playerVisibleSurface: The company panel — who travels with you and what each one is to you.
company.js.whatMakesItFire: Recruiting a met NPC, and — SNG-175 — a bonded teacher's next-step offer now surfaces through teachersForGM. The gate existed for levels; the initiative did not until then.

reputation.js.purpose: Deeds are the source of truth and reputation is a VIEW over them — a community's opinion is the sum of the deeds it knows about.
reputation.js.playerVisibleSurface: The standing list on the character screen (band per people). Empty for most players today — the gap BATCH-12 §3 exists to close.
reputation.js.whatMakesItFire: A deed is recorded (GM deeds op or an encounter), and the standing list reads the view. Deeds fire heavily; the mark-ops that would move people-standing do not (SNG-179).

progression.js.purpose: Character growth with numbers you can point at — sub-attributes, levelling, ability acquisition and the receipts for each.
progression.js.playerVisibleSurface: Level-up prompts, the attribute allocation screen, new-ability offers, and the ✦ receipt lines when something is earned.
progression.js.whatMakesItFire: A resolved action that crosses a threshold, a level-up, or a GM offerAcquisition/offerPromotion — the player commits on their own screen (Law 9).

state.js.purpose: Content-pack loading and save/load — localStorage is authoritative, GitHub sync is optional on top.
state.js.playerVisibleSurface: Boot ("Loading the Valley"), the save/load controls, and the recovery-slot list when a save fails to write.
state.js.whatMakesItFire: Boot loads content; every save/load and the recovery path fire it. Always-on infrastructure.

generate.js.purpose: The ONE generative path — authors a new NPC, location or arc in-grain for its locale, validates against the derived schema, resolves before minting so it never duplicates, and persists it.
generate.js.playerVisibleSurface: Any place or person that did not exist until the fiction needed it; the player meets these as ordinary world content.
generate.js.whatMakesItFire: The fiction naming a person or place that does not exist yet — a GM generateRequest, or the mint-on-move path. Never eager.

places.js.purpose: Location permanence — durable changes, things left behind and discoveries that OUTLIVE the scene, fed back on every return visit.
places.js.playerVisibleSurface: The location panel's "what happened here" lines on returning somewhere you have been.
places.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM placeUpdates op writes durable change; returning to a place reads it back. SNG-176 added recall keyed to the QUESTION, not only to where you stand.

claude.js.purpose: Anthropic API transport, with MODEL_MAP as the single source of truth for task→model routing and a token/stop_reason log per call.
claude.js.playerVisibleSurface: NONE directly — but every latency the player feels and every API error card comes from here.
claude.js.whatMakesItFire: Every callClaude — the play loop, ask-the-GM, generation, world-tick. NONE player-facing directly; it is the transport.

worldmap.js.purpose: The data-driven map foundation: stable auto-positioning for every location including ones minted mid-play, plus the three zoom tiers.
worldmap.js.playerVisibleSurface: The map screen and its World / Region / Location tier bar.
worldmap.js.whatMakesItFire: Opening the map (any of three tiers), and SNG-180 wired walkingDays to the place card so the world scale is met on travel.

intent.js.purpose: Intent confirmation for costly acts — nothing irreversible or world-scale commits until the player confirms, the same shape character creation already uses.
intent.js.playerVisibleSurface: The confirmation card before a harmful or departing act, naming the cost in plain language.
intent.js.whatMakesItFire: A costly or irreversible act reaching resolution — the confirmation card is what makes it fire, and it commits nothing until the player says yes.

waygate.js.purpose: The waygate network and its hub at the Crossing, including routing a GM's moveTo to a real gate rather than letting it mint a destination.
waygate.js.playerVisibleSurface: The waygate travel control and the destination list when standing at a gate.
waygate.js.whatMakesItFire: Standing at a gate (the travel control), and a GM moveTo that names a gate is routed through it rather than minting a destination.

gm.js.purpose: Assembles the GM prompt and parses the structured reply — code owns the rules and the dice, the model owns the words.
gm.js.playerVisibleSurface: All narration and the choice buttons; also the salvage path that keeps prose on screen when the reply parses badly.
gm.js.whatMakesItFire: Every turn — it assembles the prompt and parses the reply. The one module that fires on literally every beat.

substrate.js.purpose: The SECOND difficulty map — the nanite lattice varies in density by place and each tradition is tuned to a band of it, so where you stand changes what you can do.
substrate.js.playerVisibleSurface: The success-chance readout before acting (the penalty is folded in, per SNG-116) and the thin/crowded lattice note the GM works into prose. No named receipt of its own.
substrate.js.whatMakesItFire: An ABILITY action at a place (weapon actions are substrate-free, SNG-089). The success-chance readout and the thin/crowded note are the surfaces; SNG-183 note — the geographic FIELD is precomputed at load but the ruler question is open.

practice.js.purpose: Competency as the residue of attention — an engine-owned ledger of ability use and co-activation that unlocks ranks at zero skill-point cost.
practice.js.playerVisibleSurface: Aspiration progress and the unlock notices when practice crosses a threshold.
practice.js.whatMakesItFire: Every ability use records to the ledger; an aspiration or rank threshold crossing surfaces the unlock. SNG-173 made the toolkit's quiet-craft pool recency-based so it stops draining.

party.js.purpose: Shared-world play — scene publication, the open-scene index, and merge-safe writes so two players in one scene cannot clobber each other.
party.js.playerVisibleSurface: The party beat feed and the presence indicator when someone else is in your scene.
party.js.whatMakesItFire: Publishing a scene beat in a shared session, and the presence indicator when another player is in your scene.

gm_registry.js.purpose: The declared table of everything the GM can be told — one row per context block, iterated at every call site so a capability cannot be built and left unreachable.
gm_registry.js.playerVisibleSurface: NONE. It is the §23 wiring contract itself; the player meets it only as a GM that knows things.
gm_registry.js.whatMakesItFire: Every GM call — assembleGMContext iterates it at all four call sites. NONE player-facing; the player meets it only as a GM that knows things. SNG-182 also resolves name tokens here.

standing.js.purpose: How a people regards you — seeded at creation from who you are, drifting with the company you keep, and moved by narrated acts the engine adjudicates rather than the model.
standing.js.playerVisibleSurface: The standing list on the Chronicle (band per people and settlement), the ✦ receipt when a band changes, and the GM's own knowledge of your welcome.
standing.js.whatMakesItFire: Seeded at character creation; drips per in-game day from the company you keep; a GM standingOps op moves it from a narrated act. BATCH-12 §3 — before it, only a structured quest with a people-effect ever wrote peopleDisposition, which is why Erik's standing screen sat empty.

names.js.purpose: One name, one home — a display name lives on the record its id points at, and every reference resolves it rather than spelling it.
names.js.playerVisibleSurface: Every authored name a player reads: a rename changes the record and the prose follows, and SNG-111's progressive naming renders the same person differently to someone who has learned their name.
names.js.whatMakesItFire: Prompt assembly and any render of authored prose — a {{kind:id}} token resolves to the current display name. Always, but a no-op on strings without a token.

affiliation.js.purpose: The one answer to 'what kind of being is this person, and what do they practise' — read from their own role, skills and region with provenance, so the two mint paths can never disagree about it again.
affiliation.js.playerVisibleSurface: Indirect: every teacher who can now teach and every people who can now credit you — a met NPC's craft becoming legible is what lets standing move and a mentor mentor.
affiliation.js.whatMakesItFire: Meeting anyone in play (npcs.js applyNpcUpdates), minting anyone (generate.js), and the reconcile v11 backfill over people already in the save. Before SNG-185 the meet-path stamped nothing, which is why registry-only people had no craft.

devcapture.js.purpose: See the machine: a dev-only ring buffer holding the last two-dozen model exchanges — assembled prompt, raw response, parsed result, ops fired — so the by-hand SNG-179 diagnosis (read the prompt, read the raw output, see what the engine did) is a standing instrument, not a devtools dig.
devcapture.js.playerVisibleSurface: None for a player — it is dev-mode-only (the 🔬 Machine screen behind isDevMode()). Its value is that a defect that used to cost a live play session to find is now visible in a panel.
devcapture.js.whatMakesItFire: callClaude invokes the observer app.js registers ONLY under isDevMode() at boot; every model call (GM turn + intent-parse + narrate + generate) routes through that one seam. In a player view the observer is null and nothing is recorded (§3.4).

assignments.js.purpose: Hold DELEGATED WORK as state so the world turns instead of narrating: a named person put in charge of ongoing work is an assignment the tick ADVANCES (progress/stall/problem/done), and a charge set against a crisis is the mechanism that can push the crisis back — the inversion of asking what a person merely felt.
assignments.js.playerVisibleSurface: The return-tick's news reports what MOVED on the work (a problem hit, a charge finished, a crisis EASED/HELD); the DELEGATED WORK block lets the GM speak to the commitments; the person's statusNote carries the colour.
assignments.js.whatMakesItFire: delegateOps (the player puts a known person in charge of ongoing work) writes an assignment; runWorldTick advances them on return and lets progressAgainst a crisis counter its worsening. No delegation → no assignments, and an empty news block is legitimate.

latentarcs.js.purpose: The world's OWN agenda: latent arcs foment on the world count whether or not the player has seen them, and surface at thresholds as first contact — so discovery is a late event in something that has been building, and the world is not hero-dependent. Every arc carries a cause that existed before it surfaced.
latentarcs.js.playerVisibleSurface: The return-tick surfaces an arc as a rumour now specific enough to repeat, or reports one that resolved itself without you; a surfaced arc rides in the STIRRING IN THE WORLD block for the GM to develop into a face, a changed place, or a thread.
latentarcs.js.whatMakesItFire: runGenerationTurn (in worldtick.js) runs on the world count alongside the return-tick — seeds new arcs from the disposition of the regions the player knows, foments the existing ones, and surfaces those past the threshold. No known regions → nothing seeds; an arc may resolve or grow with no player involvement.

codex.js.purpose: The character's knowledge graph — what they have LEARNED as linked, typed topics (mysteries, factions, lore, people, places) with accumulating facts, written only through clamped ops.
codex.js.playerVisibleSurface: The codex screen and the CODEX block, which lets the GM have the character ACT on what they know instead of re-explaining it.
codex.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM codexUpdates op writes a topic or fact; auto-consolidation (SNG-153) folds duplicates past a threshold; the codex screen reads it.

resolve.js.purpose: Deterministic d100 action resolution — the model never rolls; this turns an action and its modifiers into a rolled degree of success the whole engine reads. Pure.
resolve.js.playerVisibleSurface: Indirect but constant: the success-chance readout before acting and the outcome degree the narration is handed. No panel of its own — it is the dice under everything.
resolve.js.whatMakesItFire: Every action that resolves — and every encounter, gambit and skill-battle round routes its roll through here.

genschema.js.purpose: A dependency-free JSON-Schema checker and repair helper for the generative pipeline — exactly the subset the derived npc/location/arc schemas use, so a grown entity is shaped like an authored one.
genschema.js.playerVisibleSurface: NONE directly — it validates generation; the player meets it only as generated content that never arrives malformed.
genschema.js.whatMakesItFire: A generateRequest — generate.js validates (and auto-repairs) the model's output against the derived schema before minting.

inventory.js.purpose: Items as first-class objects the character can carry and USE — normalized losslessly from legacy string saves, with quantities, uses and dedupe.
inventory.js.playerVisibleSurface: The inventory panel and the INVENTORY block; the receipt when an item is used or consumed.
inventory.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM itemUpdates op writes the pack; using an item in a scene reads and decrements it.

personalArc.js.purpose: Every character's PRIMARY arc, generated from the backstory they wrote — the bound-arc shape SNG-132's runtime surfaces and paces, so a player's own throughline is a real quest that is never foreclosed.
personalArc.js.playerVisibleSurface: The personal-arc quest in STRUCTURED QUESTS, surfaced and paced like any bound arc.
personalArc.js.whatMakesItFire: Character creation generates it from the bio; a beat that touches it advances it; reconcile backfills it onto older saves.

sync.js.purpose: GitHub transport for the shared world, swappable by design — nothing outside this file knows the world lives on GitHub. Character/profile backup, ledger append, shared-scene primitives.
sync.js.playerVisibleSurface: The save-sync status and the shared-world writes; localStorage stays authoritative and this is the optional layer on top.
sync.js.whatMakesItFire: A save/backup, a ledger append, or a shared-scene write in a co-op session.

canon.js.purpose: Shared family canon — a personal-canon entity a player grew real EARNS its way into a shared store the whole world reads, contradictions RANKED by realness rather than rejected. Pure.
canon.js.playerVisibleSurface: The SHARED WORLD CANON block — other players' promoted figures, places and threads the GM may reference as established.
canon.js.whatMakesItFire: A nominated entity's weight crossing the promotion threshold (the threshold IS the gate; no human curator). The sync orchestration that carries it across the repo lives in worldtick.js.

companions.js.purpose: Traveling companions — present in every scene, voiced by the GM, mechanically helpful where their nature fits; the character carries only the ids, definitions are content.
companions.js.playerVisibleSurface: The COMPANIONS block and the companion detail card — who travels with you and what they can do.
companions.js.whatMakesItFire: Always, for a present companion; an evolution threshold deepens one (SNG-175).

functions.js.purpose: The function-family axis over the skill system — inverts the authored verb→family vocabulary (8 families, 24 verbs), measures a character's coverage, and recommends skills that fill the gap.
functions.js.playerVisibleSurface: The function-gap line in the toolkit block, and the coverage read where a kit's missing family is named.
functions.js.whatMakesItFire: Assembling the toolkit (the gap), and any place the engine reasons about what KIND of act an ability performs.

legends.js.purpose: The world's great figures — heroes witnessed and villains scaling from riffraff to arc, so a world feels large when greatness passes through it. The engine detects the beat and GOVERNS the rarity; the GM narrates.
legends.js.playerVisibleSurface: The LEGEND block — a great figure surfacing in the fiction, governed so it stays rare.
legends.js.whatMakesItFire: A qualifying beat (maybeLegendDetail governs the rarity); rides the generate + weight/recurrence + codex machinery.

reconcile.js.purpose: Save and content migration — versioned, idempotent, one-time steps that bring a character or content authored by an older engine up to current, so nobody loses a save to a schema change.
reconcile.js.playerVisibleSurface: The one-time '✦ …' notices on load when a migration grants a character what a newer feature owes it.
reconcile.js.whatMakesItFire: Loading a save (or content) whose reconcile-version trails the engine — each step runs once, in order, then never again.

skill_battle.js.purpose: The two-sided contest — both wills declare a skill and intensity and roll through resolve.js; the higher net wins the round and shifts a bidirectional momentum meter, with energy attrition the second win condition.
skill_battle.js.playerVisibleSurface: The skill-battle receipt — momentum, each side's real math (the opponent's roll fogged by sense.js until earned), and the win.
skill_battle.js.whatMakesItFire: Entering a structured skill duel (SNG-098); the recurrence engine's prestige challengers resolve through it.

worldtime.js.purpose: The two clocks — character time (days and season, the player's own) and the world Kept Count (a shared, monotonic mark spoken in the local people's idiom), in different units so neither invents a date for the other.
worldtime.js.playerVisibleSurface: The CURRENT TIME narration and the count rendered in the local idiom; the story/real time-mode setting.
worldtime.js.whatMakesItFire: Always — readClock runs every turn and the count advances about once per real hour; SNG-191 removed the invented day-number.

corrections.js.purpose: The game self-heals — a bounded stateOps the GM proposes and the engine validates and applies to the asking player's OWN save. REPAIR, not wish: fix data that is wrong, never grant what play did not earn.
corrections.js.playerVisibleSurface: The POSSIBLE ERROR advisory in the prompt and the Repair panel; the ✦ receipt when a correction lands.
corrections.js.whatMakesItFire: An anomaly detected at assembly (detectAnomalies), or the player opening the Repair panel — the GM emits stateOps, the engine adjudicates.

encounters.js.purpose: Typed multi-round structures — duels, challenges, puzzles — that CONSUME the d100 resolution and map receipts onto encounter state through data tables; the GM narrates the state, never advances it.
encounters.js.playerVisibleSurface: The ACTIVE ENCOUNTER receipt each round and the AVAILABLE ENCOUNTERS the GM may offer here.
encounters.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM newEncounter/encounterOps, or firing one of the location's available encounters; each round consumes an ordinary resolve.js roll.

evolution.js.purpose: Living gear — an item that wakes through stages as a companion bond and the item×companion co-use count cross thresholds; the stage the engine owns, never the model.
evolution.js.playerVisibleSurface: The LIVING GEAR block and the AN ITEM WAKES beat when a stage unlocks.
evolution.js.whatMakesItFire: A stage's unlockBond and unlockCoUse both met through play — the engine advances the stage, the GM narrates the change.

facts.js.purpose: A durable, non-scrolling ledger of load-bearing facts (a rescue, a death, a promise) — unlike the windowed chronicle, the whole active ledger is fed every turn as truth the GM must never contradict.
facts.js.playerVisibleSurface: The ESTABLISHED FACTS block — persistent, authoritative truths regardless of how many scenes have passed.
facts.js.whatMakesItFire: A GM factUpdates op adds or resolves a fact; every turn the active ledger is carried into the prompt.

narration_voice.js.purpose: The narration's voice for table play — the place's earned register (concrete↔poetic) and the read-aloud constraint, plus a picker that prefers the good installed voices over the default worst one.
narration_voice.js.playerVisibleSurface: The NARRATIVE REGISTER and READ ALOUD blocks, the Settings read-aloud toggle, and the ▶ speak control's voice.
narration_voice.js.whatMakesItFire: Always (the register colours every beat); the read-aloud directive when spoken play is on.

sense.js.purpose: The graduated sense-of-success filter — the engine always knows the true odds; the character perceives only what their attunement earns, and an opponent's roll is fogged until sense is high enough. Pure.
sense.js.playerVisibleSurface: The precision of the success-chance readout (a vague versus exact odds read) and the fog over an opponent's roll in a skill battle.
sense.js.whatMakesItFire: Any success preview and any skill-battle round — attunement gates how much of the true math is shown.

toolkit.js.purpose: The 'what you could reach for' summary — a compact, recency-weighted hold-up of the player's OWN crafts, combines, aspiration, carried items and function-gap, so the GM can offer ONE, lightly.
toolkit.js.playerVisibleSurface: The TOOLKIT block — options the player may have forgotten, surfaced as a light offer (rule 16B), never a menu.
toolkit.js.whatMakesItFire: Assembled each turn; SNG-173 made its quiet-craft pool recency-based so long-unused crafts fade rather than crowd.

affinities.js.purpose: How a place shapes a roll, both ways and both legible — a TYPE affinity (a tag's flat bump to matching skills) and a VECTOR alignment (an ability whose axes agree with the place's strong spectrum).
affinities.js.playerVisibleSurface: The affinity receipt line — why THIS place helped or resisted the action.
affinities.js.whatMakesItFire: An action at a place — the flat and vector modifiers are computed and surfaced on resolve.

backfill.js.purpose: One-time retroactive credit for characters made before engine-XP, bonds and the practice ledger existed — a principled, CAPPED reconstruction from each character's own durable record, never an exact replay.
backfill.js.playerVisibleSurface: The one-time credit a pre-feature character receives on load (XP, bonds, practice), shown as its receipts.
backfill.js.whatMakesItFire: Loading a character created before a feature existed, once — the same versioned, idempotent discipline reconcile.js generalizes.

chronicle.js.purpose: The Chronicle reads the accreted self back to the player — one assembled page that WITNESSES who the character has become, from deeds, bonds, standing and the grown arc.
chronicle.js.playerVisibleSurface: The Chronicle screen and the CHRONICLE block — the character's story so far, plus a one-paragraph 'story so far' synthesis.
chronicle.js.whatMakesItFire: A scene closing appends a line; opening the Chronicle assembles the page (app.js owns the async synthesis call).

entityDetail.js.purpose: ONE formatter for the hover/tap detail of the three things that recur everywhere — a skill, an NPC name, an item — so the detail reads identically at every render site. Pure.
entityDetail.js.playerVisibleSurface: The detail card that appears wherever a skill, name or item is shown — the same shape everywhere.
entityDetail.js.whatMakesItFire: Opening any entity's detail — the app gathers the live values and this formats them.

gambit.js.purpose: Declared multi-step plans — the player lays out a sequence of intended actions with fallbacks, sees what their experience reveals of each step's odds, and executes; steps resolve in order through the same deterministic resolver.
gambit.js.playerVisibleSurface: The gambit builder (goal, steps, fallbacks) and the step-by-step run receipts.
gambit.js.whatMakesItFire: The player asking to plan — a gambitOps op opens the builder pre-filled; a failed step blocks the run per its fallback.

intensity.js.purpose: The three intensities a craft resolves at — Conserve / Standard / Surge — scaling energy and effect, with backlash risk carried by Surge alone; AUTO picks the minimum the task needs and never auto-surges.
intensity.js.playerVisibleSurface: The intensity choice on an ability action and the backlash receipt when a surge costs.
intensity.js.whatMakesItFire: An ability action with an intensity setting — surge is always the player's deliberate choice.

pacing.js.purpose: Scene rhythm — the world PUSHES when a beat goes quiet (pressure, escalating and place-respecting) and OFFERS when there is room (SNG-194), so the player never has to ask the world to be interesting. Pure.
pacing.js.playerVisibleSurface: The woven THE WORLD ACTS pressure beat and the THERE IS ROOM offer cue — both in-fiction, never a system announcement.
pacing.js.whatMakesItFire: Quiet turns crossing a threshold (pressure); roomForAnOffer computing a positive opening off cooldown (offer).

random_encounters.js.purpose: The living valley — on travel, rest, arrival or world-tick the engine MAY roll one flavored encounter, weighted by the place's dangerLevel and tags, across a tonal spectrum that is never grim by default.
random_encounters.js.playerVisibleSurface: The woven SOMETHING HAPPENS beat when a roll lands.
random_encounters.js.whatMakesItFire: A travel/rest/arrival/tick roll (SNG-075) — weighted by the location, and never announced as a system event.

recurrence.js.purpose: Prestige-driven recurring challengers — a bound arc draws duel challengers as the character's renown rises, the swordsman's paradox as an engine: prestige feeds challengers feeds prestige.
recurrence.js.playerVisibleSurface: A returning challenger arriving in the fiction, scaled to your renown band.
recurrence.js.whatMakesItFire: A renown (deeds-driven reputation) band crossing plus a challenger cooldown — the duel runs as an ordinary SNG-098 skill battle.

vectors.js.purpose: Makes a location's spectrum character legible to the PLAYER — a place's strong axes are felt after a visit, subtler axes need attunement or a perceiving ability, and precursor address_sense reveals all.
vectors.js.playerVisibleSurface: The place's felt axes shown to the player (the GM already has the full vector); SNG-011 progressive legibility.
vectors.js.whatMakesItFire: Visiting a place, using a perceiving ability there, or precursor address_sense — each reveals more of the vector.

worldtick.js.purpose: The world moves while you are away — on re-entry after in-game days pass, active events advance on their own clocks, delegated work progresses and arcs ferment, so a crisis ignored worsens and time away is time the world used.
worldtick.js.playerVisibleSurface: The away-digest news on return — what advanced, what a delegated crew did, what stirred in the world.
worldtick.js.whatMakesItFire: Re-entering play after in-game days have passed (runWorldTick plus the SNG-191 generation turn on the world count).
